purpose: Replayable proof that the trigger spine (trigger_check.py + trigger_registry.json, PR#15) and the caps-based sizing block (directive_recs.py, PR#18) actually catch the documented misses they were built for. Each expressible scenario feeds a synthetic quote series through trigger_check.evaluate() and asserts the trigger fires exactly once, emits a push payload, and is idempotent. The parabolic miss is recorded as a coverage gap because no current condition type expresses acceleration.

condition_type_coverage._gap: mu-parabolic-2026-05-27 needs a condition type the spine does not have

scenarios.documented_miss: The operator re-entry zone 65-70 existed in the thesis, price traded back into it on 5/5 and again on 5/12, and no ping fired -- the system knew but nobody was told.
scenarios.signal_that_existed: Live thesis re-entry zone 65-70 (intraday range touch).
scenarios.trigger_that_should_fire: level_touch on the 65-70 zone fires the moment the intraday range overlaps it.

scenarios.documented_miss: Fundstrat/Newton REIT breakout layer: an EWRE weekly close above 38 confirms the breakout. The level existed but no tactical ping fired when it printed.
scenarios.signal_that_existed: Stated tactical level: EWRE weekly close above 38.

scenarios.documented_miss: Supplemental real-registry case (the prompt names GOOGL tranche-2 as a real registry value to use). The staged tranche-2 review is a date_event; without the spine it relies on someone remembering the date.
scenarios.signal_that_existed: Staged tranche-2 review date 2026-06-19 from the live GOOGL thesis.

scenarios.documented_miss: The generic 6/9 'the system knew but nobody pinged' case. The exact signal was not pinned to one registry row, so this is a representative reconstruction encoded as an IV re-check (the prompt lists IV as a candidate signal). It is deliberately routed through iv_threshold so that condition type -- otherwise exercised by no test in the suite -- is also locked against silent regression.
scenarios.signal_that_existed: An armed re-check that should fire once event IV bleeds out (iv_rank falls below 30), signalling a cleaner entry.

scenarios.documented_miss: MU went parabolic on 5/27 (rapid multi-day price acceleration / blow-off). parabolic_setup_screener.py can score the SETUP and even classify a 'Phase 3 (parabola)', but that screener is a research surface, not a trigger condition type. The spine has no way to ARM a trigger that fires on acceleration, so nothing pinged.
scenarios.signal_that_existed: Multi-day parabolic acceleration (e.g. a sharp N-day percent move / blow-off), detectable by the parabolic screener's phase classifier.
scenarios.trigger_that_should_fire: A condition that fires on rate-of-change / acceleration, not a static level.
scenarios.why_no_existing_type_fits: price_cross / level_touch fire on a fixed price; iv_threshold fires on an IV level; date_event fires on a calendar date. None express 'price accelerated X% over N days' or 'entered Phase 3', so a parabolic move can only be caught by hard-coding a guessed price level after the fact.
